reasoning: Data from the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) and other sources indicate that bank account ownership and formal financial inclusion remained well below the 95% target by the 2024 deadline. In 2023, only about 52% of adults had bank accounts, and overall formal financial inclusion was around 64%. Furthermore, by July 2025, the number of Bank Verification Number (BVN) holders was only 66.2 million—equivalent to slightly over 50% of the adult population and far short of the ~105+ million required to hit the 95% threshold. Recent reporting confirms that Nigeria missed this ambition. As there is zero chance the 95% target was reached before Jan 1, 2025, the minimum allowable probability is selected.
batch_queries: Will 95% or more of Nigerians have a bank account before 2025?
